claim: Claude Skills are modular, task-specific instructions that execute only when needed, unlike global Custom Instructions that apply everywhere.
actionable: Use Claude Skills to encapsulate task-specific workflows like report generation instead of cluttering Custom Instructions with global rules.
source_title: Claude Skills Explained: Beginner to Advanced Tutorial

claim: Skills can be chained together in workflows alongside MCPs to build complete automation: fetch data from MCP → apply custom skill → generate branded report → send via email.
actionable: Design Skills as modular components that can be combined with MCPs and other Skills into end-to-end workflows rather than monolithic solutions.
source_title: Claude Skills Explained: Beginner to Advanced Tutorial

claim: LLMs struggle with PowerPoint generation requiring charts, tables, and complex visuals; they excel at text-based reports (Word, PDF).
actionable: Use Claude Skills for text-heavy outputs (reports, analyses) but delegate visual design tasks (PowerPoint with graphics) to specialized tools.
source_title: Claude Skills Explained: Beginner to Advanced Tutorial

claim: Skills should include reference materials (templates, brand guidelines) as attachments in the markdown file to constrain and guide output generation.
actionable: Embed reference files in your Skill markdown (brand templates, color palettes, slide patterns) so Claude replicates your style consistently.
source_title: Claude Skills Explained: Beginner to Advanced Tutorial

claim: Embedding hard references to slide structures in Skills limits flexibility because they become the only templates available; output becomes constrained to those patterns.
actionable: Balance reference specificity with flexibility: provide examples but allow variation; avoid making reference slides the absolute constraint.
source_title: Claude Skills Explained: Beginner to Advanced Tutorial

claim: Toggling off unused Skills saves tokens and reduces execution time by not loading unnecessary instructions.
actionable: Regularly audit and disable Skills you don't use in a particular workflow to minimize token overhead.
source_title: Claude Skills Explained: Beginner to Advanced Tutorial

claim: Duplicate intent across Skills (e.g., both brand guidelines and custom brand skills) can cause confusion and color palette conflicts.
actionable: Audit Skills for overlapping intentions and disable competing Skills in any single prompt to prevent conflicting outputs.
source_title: Claude Skills Explained: Beginner to Advanced Tutorial

claim: Use descriptive naming and semantic versioning (e.g., skill_v1, skill_v2) for Skills to track iterations and manage multiple versions over time.
actionable: Name Skills semantically with versioning so you can identify improvements and maintain a clear lineage of iterations.
source_title: Claude Skills Explained: Beginner to Advanced Tutorial

claim: Community Skills from GitHub require security review; treat them like code from untrusted sources and validate before using in production workflows.
actionable: Audit community Skills from GitHub before uploading, checking for prompt injection vectors or hidden behaviors.
source_title: Claude Skills Explained: Beginner to Advanced Tutorial
